# Generated by ContextEngine — npx @strifero/contextengine
# https://github.com/strifero/ContextEngine

# Project Context

## Stack
- Node.js + Express
- TypeScript (strict mode)
- PostgreSQL (via node-postgres or Prisma)
- Jest for testing

## Architecture
- `src/routes/` — Express route handlers
- `src/controllers/` — Business logic, separated from routing
- `src/services/` — Data access and external integrations
- `src/middleware/` — Auth, validation, error handling
- `src/types/` — Shared TypeScript types

## Conventions

### Structure
- Keep routes thin — delegate logic to controllers and services
- Controllers handle HTTP concerns (request parsing, response formatting)
- Services handle business logic and data access
- One file per resource (users.routes.ts, users.controller.ts, users.service.ts)

### Error handling
- Use a centralized error handler middleware registered last
- Throw typed errors from controllers/services, catch at the middleware boundary
- Never send raw error messages to clients in production

### TypeScript
- Strict mode enabled
- Type all Express request bodies with custom interfaces
- Use `zod` or `joi` for runtime validation before trusting request data

### Testing
- Unit test services in isolation (mock the database layer)
- Integration test routes against a test database
- Use `supertest` for HTTP-level route testing

## What to avoid
- Do not put business logic in route handlers
- Do not trust `req.body` without validation
- Do not use `any` — if a type is unknown, say so explicitly
